MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being assigned from Department 206 for commencement of short cause trial/prove-up hearing.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 01:57:20 p.m., pausing at 02:04:40 p.m., reconvening at 02:05:55 p.m., and concluding at 02:36:43 p.m.

Plaintiff FB Global Properties represented by Thomas Foran, Esq.
Defendant Chris Diaz not appearing.
Defendant Anthony Cura aka Anton Cura, Individually and DBA San Francisco Barber Shop not appearing.
Defendant Diana Cura, Individually and DBA San Francisco Barber Shop not appearing.

DAVID KIMMEL (Manager for FB Global Properties) is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents case summary and an offer of proof.
DAVID KIMMEL is under direct examination and examined by the Court.

Court determines monthly status of rent due by Defendants are as follows:

1. April 2020 = Rent $10,153.00; CAM (Common Area Maintenance) $756.28; Amount paid by Defendants $5,832.80

2. May 2020 = Rent $10,153.00; CAM (Common Area Maintenance) $756.28; Amount paid by Defendants $1,032.80

Court to make determination and calculation of damages to determine what is reasonable.

Hearing held. Mr. Knox states that he has been unable to get in touch with the process server, Mr. Ameer Aziz. Mr. McLeod states that defendant Diana Cura is unable to attend the hearing due to a dental surgery scheduled for today. The parties request a continuance so that all parties can be present for the hearing. The court grants the parties request for a continuance and rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS DIANA CURA AND ANTHONY CURA'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT is continued to March 16, 2022.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
